name: Lancer
target_customer: developers running agents on their own machines/servers, power users with >1 host or >1 provider, small teams with audit needs
positioning: governance layer (policy + hash-chained audit + emergency stop + fleet drift) for agents across providers, on your own machines, over an E2E-encrypted relay
pricing: unfinalized; prior internal planning mentioned ~$14.99 one-time IAP, not committed
distribution_model: TestFlight (pre-launch); App Store submission not started
architecture: iOS app + resident Go daemon (lancerd) on user's host + hosted relay/APNs (push-backend); E2E blind relay is V1 primary transport, SSH legacy/secondary
security_model: TOFU host keys, Keychain-stored credentials, fail-closed policy engine, hash-chained audit, E2E relay (server can't read); biometric gate REMOVED for V1 as of 2026-07-01 (regression)
status: active, pre-launch, strategic direction under a validation-cycle gate since 2026-06-24

name: Omnara
oss_status: archived-oss (repo archived 2026-02-02, pivoted to closed-source Claude Agent SDK rebuild)
target_customer: solo devs/vibe-coders, voice-commute crowd, prosumer wave
positioning: command center for your coding agents; monitor/steer/approve Claude Code and Codex from phone/web/voice
pricing: free tier 10 sessions/mo; paid $9/mo (Aug 2025) -> $20/mo (Feb 2026); enterprise custom
distribution_model: App Store + Google Play + web + desktop
architecture: headless local daemon holds outbound authenticated WebSocket to Omnara's cloud servers; cloud-sandbox continuity when host offline (git-commit-per-turn sync)
security_model: no true E2EE (founder-admitted); plaintext conversations/diffs stored server-side (Supabase, encrypted in transit/at rest only); no SOC2/ISO, no pen-test, no non-US data residency; 3rd-party API keys stay client-side
status: active, inflected momentum (strong 2025 launch, Feb-2026 OSS-archival + price-hike suggests strategic reset)

name: Claude Code Remote Control
positioning: drive a local Claude Code session from phone/browser; code never leaves the machine
pricing: bundled free with Pro ($20/mo) / Max plans; off-by-default admin-gated on Team/Enterprise
architecture: local claude remote-control process registers with Anthropic API over outbound HTTPS, no inbound ports; multi-session server mode up to 32 concurrent, git-worktree isolation
security_model: outbound-only HTTPS, no inbound ports, short-lived scoped credentials, claude.ai OAuth only (no API keys)
last_release_date: 2026 (near-weekly version bumps, e.g. push notif at v2.1.110, /config over mobile at v2.1.181)

name: Codex in ChatGPT mobile
positioning: control Codex sessions on your own machines from the ChatGPT mobile app; queue/steer/plan, approve commands, review diffs
pricing: available on all plans including Free and Go tier
architecture: remote connections via secure relay; host = macOS/Windows Codex App; local-first execution, files/creds/permissions stay on host
security_model: sandboxed execution (Seatbelt macOS / bwrap+seccomp Linux / WSL2 or native Windows), network off by default with domain allowlist; Face ID/passcode lock for Codex separately

name: GitHub Copilot CLI Remote Control + Agent HQ
supported_agents: Copilot coding agent + Agent HQ rolling out third-party: Anthropic, OpenAI, Google, Cognition, xAI
positioning: stream a local CLI session and approve/deny permission requests (tool/file/URL) from GitHub Mobile, incl. non-GitHub repos/local dirs; Agent HQ = unified cross-vendor mobile mission control
distribution_model: GitHub Mobile app + web + VS Code + CLI
architecture: streams local CLI session to mobile; Agent HQ brings third-party agents under one consistent interface across GitHub/VS Code/mobile/CLI
security_model: tool/file-path/URL-scoped permission prompts; cannot self-merge/self-approve destructive actions
status: active; Agent HQ partly rolling out over following months (mixed GA/announced)

name: Tactic Remote
target_customer: individual developers wanting phone control of Claude Code/Codex
positioning: iPhone/iPad control layer: live terminal stream, tmux persistence, file browsing, approval workflows, plan review, prompt queue, push notifications
distribution_model: unknown (vendor site only, no independent App Store confirmation found in prior pass)
architecture: reportedly Cloudflare Tunnel (TLS) + API-key auth + path sandbox
security_model: Cloudflare Tunnel + API-key auth; weaker security story than E2E-relay + TOFU + Keychain/biometric competitors, per prior analysis
status: unknown, no independent user reviews found as of 2026-06-23

name: Orca (ADE)
target_customer: power users running fleets of parallel agents
positioning: agent development environment: run a fleet of parallel agents in isolated git worktrees, desktop + iOS/Android companion to monitor/steer
architecture: undocumented in public materials as of prior pass; worktree-per-task fan-out
security_model: unknown/undocumented — a possible opening for Lancer to out-position on security
status: active, ~6.3k GitHub stars (proxy for interest, not confirmed active-user count)

name: Nimbalyst
positioning: AI-native desktop workspace (kanban for parallel Claude Code/Codex, visual editors) + iOS companion with visual diff review, push notifications, team visibility
distribution_model: requires Nimbalyst desktop host + iOS companion
architecture: desktop daemon parallel to Lancer's; iOS app requires the desktop host running
status: active; note: its own blog is comparison-marketing (bias flag when citing its rankings)

name: Happy
target_customer: users wanting lightweight Claude Code session monitoring
positioning: native iOS app: monitor Claude Code sessions, push notifications, status indicators — read-mostly, limited interaction
pricing: cited by HN commenters as having E2E encryption and being cheaper than Omnara's $20/mo
security_model: claimed E2E encryption (per HN commenter comparisons to Omnara) — not independently verified in prior pass
status: active, referenced by HN commenters as an Omnara alternative/switch target

name: Cursor (Cloud/Background Agents, Web+Mobile, Slack)
positioning: assign/steer background coding agents from a mobile browser/installable PWA; Slack @Cursor launch + notifications
pricing: tied to Cursor subscription tiers (not independently re-verified this pass)
distribution_model: installable PWA (not native app) + Slack integration
architecture: cloud VMs run the agent; PWA/Slack dispatch and monitor
security_model: cloud-hosted (Cursor's infrastructure, not user's own machine) — different trust model than Lancer's own-machine premise

name: Factory (Droids — Web & Mobile, Slack)
target_customer: teams wanting to ship from phone
positioning: approve diffs, unblock droids, leave feedback from phone (web); mobile-first review flow

name: Sourcegraph Amp
positioning: drive your agents from anywhere: web, CLI, mobile; threads sync across devices
distribution_model: unknown, marketing-page mobile claim not independently verified
status: active, lighter/less-detailed mobile claim than GitHub/Factory

name: Devin / Cognition
positioning: async cloud-VM agent, tag Devin in Slack, get DM status updates, schedule recurring sessions
distribution_model: Slack + web, no first-party mobile control app
status: active; behavioral substitute for phone-control via Slack-on-phone

name: Google Gemini CLI + Jules
positioning: async cloud-VM coding agent with plan-approval before code, suggested-tasks approve/dismiss
distribution_model: web UI (no native mobile notifications yet); unofficial community mobile client exists (linkalls/jules-mobile-client)
architecture: Jules cloud VM, multiple parallel Jules instances
status: active; native mobile notifications explicitly not yet supported per docs

name: OpenCode (sst)
target_customer: developers wanting an open local agent server
positioning: local agent client/server architecture; mobile client in beta — architecturally closest to Lancer's own daemon model
distribution_model: CLI install; mobile client beta not GA
architecture: local agent server (daemon), drivable remotely; sub-agents supported
status: active — watch closely, Lancer already dispatches to OpenCode as one of its 4 supported vendors

name: Vibe Kanban (BloopAI)
positioning: kanban for orchestrating multiple coding agents, cross-vendor, fully local
architecture: local worktrees, parallel execution, visual diff review
status: shut-down (parent company Bloop shut down 2026-04-10 citing 'no viable business model'); OSS continues community-maintained

name: Locally AI / LM Link
company: Element Labs Inc (acquired by LM Studio ~2026-04-08)
supported_agents: n/a — general local LLM chat, not coding agents
positioning: run open-source LLMs on-device via MLX; LM Link extension lets phone act as E2E-encrypted client to a desktop LM Studio instance
pricing: free during preview, paid plans later (unconfirmed)
architecture: LM Link = phone-as-client to trusted desktop, E2E encrypted, chats saved locally — architecturally identical shape to Lancer's relay, applied to LLM chat not coding agents
security_model: E2E encrypted phone-to-desktop pairing (LM Link); 'your data never leaves your control' marketing, well received in reviews
status: active — best external proof-of-concept that Lancer's E2E-relay architecture is sound and monetizable

name: Blume
positioning: desktop sidecar watching multiple coding agents and managing their hidden config; not remote control, not mobile
architecture: local, on-device, watches multi-agent config drift
status: active, new, no public user feedback footprint found as of prior pass

name: DIY: Tailscale + SSH + tmux (+ Termius/Blink, mosh, ntfy)
target_customer: technical developers, exactly Lancer's target user
positioning: free VPN mesh (Tailscale) + iOS SSH client + tmux persistence, optionally mosh + ntfy push hooks
architecture: direct SSH over Tailscale mesh, tmux for durability, ntfy.sh for push notifications via a Claude Code hook
security_model: VPN-mesh trust (Tailscale), no governed approvals, no audit trail, no policy engine — manual typing of approve/deny in the TUI
status: durable, beloved by exactly Lancer's target user per multiple independent 2026 blogs
